Doctor_English: Have you been fasting with dry mouth or without water as part of your pre-operative preparation?
Patient_Yoruba: Wọ́n sọ fún mi pé kí n má mu ohunkóhun láti òru àná. Mi ò mu omi tàbí jẹun. Mo ti ń gbẹ ẹnu mi fún wákàtí mẹ́rìnlá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prolonged pre-operative fasting exceeding current enhanced recovery protocols, with water restriction beyond the recommended two-hour clear fluid fast, creating risk of dehydration, hypotension on induction, and metabolic stress, requiring updated fasting instructions.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty with your digestion, with food feeling like it is sitting in your stomach?
Patient_Yoruba: Oúnjẹ máa ń jẹ mí bíi pé ó dúró nínú ikùn mi fún wákàtí pípẹ́ lẹ́yìn jíjẹun. Mo ń ní ríru pẹ̀lú. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Gastroparesis symptoms with delayed gastric emptying sensation, nausea, and early satiety over six months, a common complication of diabetes, requiring gastric emptying scintigraphy and prokinetic therapy.

Doctor_English: How long does each episode of palpitation last, and is there anything that reliably stops it?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọkàn mi máa ń lu yára yára fún ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́wàá sí ogún. Nígbà tí mo bá gbìyànjú láti kọ́ ẹ̀gbẹ́ pẹ̀lú ọwọ́ mi, ó máa ń dáwọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Paroxysmal tachycardia lasting ten to twenty minutes with vagal manoeuvre termination by carotid sinus massage, highly specific for atrioventricular nodal re-entrant tachycardia requiring electrophysiology study and consideration of catheter ablation.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any association between your symptoms and your menstrual cycle?
Patient_Yoruba: Àrùn mi máa ń burú ní ọ̀sẹ̀ ṣáájú oṣù mi àti yẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ọjọ́ mẹ́ta ti oṣù. O ti jẹ́ bẹ́ẹ̀ fún ọdún méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Premenstrual symptom exacerbation with post-menstrual remission over two years, consistent with premenstrual exacerbation of an underlying condition such as migraine, asthma, endometriosis, or premenstrual dysphoric disorder, requiring prospective symptom diary and hormonal assessment.

Doctor_English: Is the shortness of breath worse during exercise, or does it happen even at rest?
Patient_Yoruba: Mí mi kúrú nígbà tí mo bá ń rìn jíjìn díẹ̀. Nígbà tí mo bá jókòó, ó máa ń yẹ̀ díẹ̀ ṣùgbọ́n kò lọ pátápátá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Dyspnoea on minimal exertion with incomplete relief at rest, indicating moderate to severe cardiorespiratory compromise warranting urgent functional assessment.

Doctor_English: Was the patient in a road traffic accident, and did they lose consciousness at any point?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọkọ̀ ayọ́kẹ́lẹ́ kan gbá a ní ikorita. Ó sọ pé kò mọ̀ ohun tó ṣẹlẹ̀ fún ìṣẹ́jú díẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ìjàǹbá náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Road traffic collision with a witnessed period of unconsciousness indicating traumatic brain injury, requiring cervical spine immobilisation, Glasgow Coma Scale scoring, and urgent cranial CT imaging.

Doctor_English: Have you had any investigations for recurrent miscarriages, and have you been referred to a specialist?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti sọ oyún nù lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta ní ìdajì àkọ́kọ́ oyún. Dọ́kítà sọ pé a máa ń padà wá ṣùgbọ́n ko tọ́ka wa sí ibi tó péye.
Clinical_Translation_English: Three consecutive first trimester losses without investigation or specialist referral, meeting criteria for recurrent miscarriage requiring antiphospholipid antibody testing, thrombophilia screen, parental karyotype, uterine cavity assessment, and endocrine evaluation.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling extremely tired, muscle weakness, and salt craving, with darker skin than usual?
Patient_Yoruba: Àárẹ̀ mi le gan-an, iṣan ara mi àì ní agbára, àti pé mo fẹ́ oúnjẹ iyọ̀ lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀. Awọ ara mi tún ti di dúdú sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: The triad of severe fatigue, proximal myopathy, salt craving, and hyperpigmentation raises strong concern for primary adrenal insufficiency (Addison's disease), a potentially life-threatening condition requiring urgent cortisol measurement and endocrine review.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any phantom limb pain since your amputation?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń ní irora ní ibi ẹsẹ̀ mi tó ti gé kúrò, bíi ẹni pé ó ṣì wà. Ó máa ń bani lóru ní alẹ́ àti ń dí mí lọ́wọ́ ìsùn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Phantom limb pain causing nocturnal sleep disruption post-amputation, a neuropathic pain syndrome requiring mirror therapy, graded motor imagery, gabapentinoid pharmacotherapy, and psychological support for body image adaptation.

Doctor_English: How severe is your fatigue — can you still carry out your normal household duties?
Patient_Yoruba: Àárẹ̀ mi le tó jẹ́ kí n máà lè jẹun pa àwọn ọmọ mi. Mo ń wà lórí ibùsùn fún ọjọ́ mẹ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: Severe fatigue rendering the patient bedridden for three days and unable to perform basic domestic tasks, suggesting systemic illness or haematological pathology.

Doctor_English: Have you visited any prayer house or spiritual home for healing before coming to the hospital?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo lọ sí ilé ìjọsìn fún ọjọ́ mẹ́wàá ṣáájú kí n tó wá. Wọ́n gbàdúrà fún mi àti fún mi ní omi mímọ́ láti mu. Àìsàn mi kò yẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Faith healing consultation for ten days prior to hospital presentation with consumption of blessed water of unknown composition, illustrating the frequent parallel use of spiritual and biomedical healthcare in Nigerian patients and the potential delay in diagnosis this creates.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any small red or purple spots that do not disappear when you press on them?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo rí àwọn ìwọ̀ pupa kéékèèké lórí ẹsẹ̀ mi méjèèjì tí kò ń lọ nígbà tí a bá tẹ àwọ ara wọn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Non-blanching petechiae bilaterally on the lower limbs, a critical finding indicating possible vasculitis, thrombocytopaenia, or meningococcaemia, requiring urgent full blood count and coagulation studies.

Doctor_English: Is the breast lump in the upper outer area, near the nipple, or in the armpit?
Patient_Yoruba: Èso náà wà ní ìhà ìgbẹ́yìn ìsàlẹ̀ ọmú mi òsì, jìnnà sí ọ̀tọ̀ ọmú. Ko wà ní àgbègbè ẹ̀gbẹ̀ ọwọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Lower outer quadrant breast lump, a less common location than the upper outer quadrant where most breast cancers occur, but requiring the same urgent triple assessment protocol with clinical examination, ultrasound or mammography, and core biopsy.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain in the back of your heel that gets worse when you push off to walk?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹ̀hin ìpẹ̀sẹ̀ mi ọ̀tún ń dùn nígbà tí mo bá gbé ẹsẹ̀ mi ga láti rìn. Ó burú sí i lẹ́yìn ìdárayá. O ti wà fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Posterior heel pain worsening with push-off consistent with Achilles tendinopathy requiring eccentric loading physiotherapy and load management.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have a hiatus hernia and have you changed your diet because of it?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé apá ìsàlẹ̀ ìṣan jíjẹun mi ti wọlé ikùn. Ìgbóná ń jẹ mi lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ ṣùgbọ́n mi ò yí àṣà mi padà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Hiatus hernia with reflux symptoms without lifestyle modification, requiring weight reduction, avoiding late meals, and proton pump inhibitor therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your iron is low, and are you a vegetarian or do you avoid red meat?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo jẹ oúnjẹ tí kò ní ẹran fún ọdún mẹ́ta. Dọ́kítà sọ pé irin mi kéré. Mi ò jẹ ẹja pẹ̀lú nítorí ìsìn mi. Mo jẹ ẹyin àti ewé nìkan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Iron deficiency in a strict vegetarian avoiding both meat and fish for three years on religious grounds, indicating inadequate non-haem iron intake requiring dietitian referral for plant-based iron optimisation, vitamin C co-ingestion advice, and oral iron supplementation.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any tingling or weakness in both hands that is worse at night?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọwọ́ mi méjèéjì máa ń hu bíi àbẹ̀rẹ̀ ń gún wọn ní alẹ́ tó ń jí mi dìde. Mo máa ń gbọn wọn láti gba ìtura.
Clinical_Translation_English: Bilateral nocturnal hand paraesthesia relieved by shaking, the characteristic presentation of bilateral carpal tunnel syndrome requiring nerve conduction studies and consideration of surgical decompression.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed that your breathing is worse in certain rooms of your house, such as the bedroom?
Patient_Yoruba: Mí mi máa ń kúrú sí i nínú ìyàrá ìsùn mi ju àwọn yàrá mìíràn lọ. Ní ita ilé, ó máa ń dára. Mo rò pé ó jẹ́ nítorí ìbòjú ìrọ̀lẹ́ mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Room-specific dyspnoea worst in the bedroom with outdoor improvement, consistent with exposure to house dust mites, mould spores, or pet dander in the sleeping environment, requiring allergen avoidance measures, impermeable mattress covers, and immunological assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you had any episodes of passing bright red blood directly from your back passage?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, ẹjẹ̀ pupa ni mo rí nínú ìgbẹ́ mi. Ó wà lórí ìgbẹ́ náà, kò papọ̀ mọ́ rẹ̀. Ó ti jẹ́ bẹ́ẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́rin.
Clinical_Translation_English: Fresh rectal bleeding with blood coating the stool rather than mixed within it on four occasions, a pattern more consistent with anorectal pathology such as haemorrhoids or anal fissure, though colorectal malignancy must be excluded.

Doctor_English: How severe is the fatigue — are you able to carry out any activity at all, or are you confined to bed?
Patient_Yoruba: Àárẹ̀ mi le tó jẹ́ kí n máà lè dìde lórí ibùsùn mọ́. Mo wà ní ibùsùn fún ọjọ́ mẹ́fà. Àwọn ọmọ mi ń mu oúnjẹ wá fún mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Completely bedridden fatigue for six days with total dependence for feeding, indicating profound systemic illness such as severe anaemia, malignancy, cardiac failure, or sepsis requiring comprehensive evaluation and urgent supportive care.

Doctor_English: Did the patient collapse suddenly, and was there any pulse felt at the time?
Patient_Yoruba: Ó ṣubú lójijì ní gbọ̀ngàn ilé. Àwọn ẹbí rẹ̀ gbìyànjú láti rí ohùn ọkàn ṣùgbọ́n kò rí i. Wọ́n pè mí lẹ́sẹ̀kẹsẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Sudden witnessed collapse with absent pulse reported by bystanders, consistent with cardiac arrest requiring immediate cardiopulmonary resuscitation and advanced life support activation.

Doctor_English: Has anyone in your household been diagnosed with lung disease, and do they smoke indoors?
Patient_Yoruba: Bàbá mi ní àrùn ẹ̀dọ̀fóró àti ń mu sìgá nínú ilé. A ń gbé ní yàrá kéékèéké. A ń mí èéfín rẹ̀ ní gbogbo ọjọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Chronic indoor secondhand smoke exposure from a household member with lung disease in a crowded dwelling, constituting a significant respiratory hazard for all household members and requiring smoking cessation intervention and ventilation improvement.

Doctor_English: How severe is the muscle pain after exercise — does it stop you from moving your limbs the following day?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn ìdárayá, iṣan ara mi ń dùn tó jẹ́ kí n máà lè gbé apá mi ní ọjọ́ tó tẹ̀le. Ó dúró fún ọjọ́ mẹ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: Exercise-induced delayed onset muscle soreness causing functional limitation for three days, beyond the typical forty-eight hour peak of normal DOMS, requiring creatine kinase measurement to exclude rhabdomyolysis, particularly if urine is dark.

Doctor_English: Has the child been having difficulty reading or keeping up with schoolwork since starting school?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi ń ṣàkíyèsí ìkọ̀wé àti kíkà lórí. Ó ń lo ìgbà pípẹ́ ju àwọn ọmọ mìíràn lọ ṣùgbọ́n kò máa ń parí. Wọ́n sọ pé ó ń yẹra fún kíkà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Learning difficulty with disproportionate effort required for reading, avoidance behaviour, and failure to complete written work, consistent with dyslexia or a specific learning disorder, requiring psychoeducational assessment and individualised educational support.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing pain in multiple joints that moves from one joint to another over days?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora máa ń wáyé ní ìpín ọwọ́ mi, lẹ́yìn ọjọ́ méjì ó máa ń lọ sí orúkún, lẹ́yìn náà sí ìbàdí. Ó ń yí padà ní ìgbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Migratory polyarthritis moving sequentially between joints over days, consistent with acute rheumatic fever, gonococcal arthritis, or viral arthritis, each requiring distinct investigation including ASOT, throat swab, and STI screen.

Doctor_English: Have you been using the same syringe more than once to inject your insulin, and how are you storing it?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń lo abẹ́rẹ̀ insulin mi lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́rin ṣáájú kí n tó pàárọ̀ rẹ̀. A fi insulin mi pamọ́ ní ìta ilé wa nítorí àpótí ìtọ́jú tutù kò ṣiṣẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Repeated use of insulin syringes and improper ambient temperature storage of insulin in a resource-limited setting, causing syringe blunting with increased injection pain, contamination risk, and insulin degradation leading to erratic glycaemic control.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have a peptic ulcer and are you taking treatment for it?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé ọgbẹ́ wà nínú ikùn mi ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. A fún mi ní oògùn fún oṣù méjì ṣùgbọ́n mi ò tẹ̀lé ìdánwò ti a ní ṣe.
Clinical_Translation_English: Peptic ulcer without post-treatment H. pylori eradication confirmation, creating relapse risk, requiring urea breath test and endoscopic review if symptoms persist.

Doctor_English: Have you ever been told your kidneys are not working well, or have you had any kidney investigations?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà kan sọ pé ìdánwò ẹjẹ̀ mi fihàn pé àwọn ẹ̀dọ̀ ito mi ti ń ṣiṣẹ́ díẹ̀ díẹ̀. Ó ní kí n padà wá ṣùgbọ́n mi ò lọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Documented renal impairment identified on prior blood testing with failure to attend follow-up, indicating possible chronic kidney disease with loss to monitoring and risk of progression to end-stage renal failure.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling very shaky, sweaty, and confused before meals, and does eating immediately help?
Patient_Yoruba: Ṣáájú oúnjẹ ọjọ́-ọjọ́, mo máa ń gbọn àti ríru. Inú mi di àásìkí díẹ̀. Nígbà tí mo bá jẹ, gbogbo rẹ̀ máa ń dára lẹ́sẹ̀kẹsẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pre-prandial autonomic and neuroglycopaenic symptoms with immediate resolution on food intake, the classic presentation of reactive hypoglycaemia requiring a supervised seventy-two hour fast and insulin-to-glucose ratio measurement to exclude insulinoma.

Doctor_English: Has anyone else in your household been having the same symptoms, suggesting a shared cause?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìyàwó mi àti ọmọ mi ọdún mẹ́wàá tún ní irora ikùn àti ìgbẹ́ gbuuru. A jẹ oúnjẹ ìpamọ́ kanna ní ọjọ́ méjì sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Cluster of gastrointestinal illness across three household members sharing the same stored food two days prior, the epidemiological pattern of a common source food poisoning outbreak requiring stool culture, food sample testing, and public health notification.

Doctor_English: Have you been gaining weight specifically around your abdomen with thin arms and legs?
Patient_Yoruba: Ikùn mi àti ìhà àyà mi ń pọ̀ sí i ṣùgbọ́n apá àti ẹsẹ̀ mi di tínrín. Awọ ara mi tún ti di tinrín àti dídán.
Clinical_Translation_English: Central adiposity with peripheral muscle wasting and skin thinning constituting a cushingoid distribution, raising concern for Cushing's syndrome requiring midnight cortisol, twenty-four hour urinary free cortisol, and dexamethasone suppression testing.

Doctor_English: Have you been getting any chest pain that is relieved immediately by glyceryl trinitrate spray?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní ìfọ̀n GTN fún ọdún méjì. Irora àyà mi máa ń lọ ní ìṣẹ́jú méjì lẹ́yìn tí mo bá lo. Mo ń nílò rẹ̀ ní ìgbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Nitrate-responsive chest pain confirming a vasospastic or ischaemic cardiac aetiology, with episodic use over two years indicating either stable angina with infrequent symptoms or worsening disease if usage is increasing, requiring exercise stress testing and cardiology review.

Doctor_English: Has your child ever had an operation or procedure under general anaesthetic, and were there any problems?
Patient_Yoruba: A ṣe iṣẹ́ abẹ fún ọmọ mi fún àmì tonsil rẹ̀ ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Ó jí déédéé lẹ́yìn àánì. Kò sí ìṣòro kankan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Uncomplicated tonsillectomy under general anaesthesia two years ago, relevant as paediatric anaesthetic history for any future procedure planning, confirming previous normal airway, recovery, and absence of prior adverse anaesthetic reactions.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite — are you eating much more or much less than usual?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò fẹ jẹun rárá fún oṣù kan. Mo ti tẹ̀nìí, àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi sì ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí ṣàkíyèsí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Significant anorexia with visible weight loss over one month noted by close contacts, a somatic manifestation of major depression or an independent eating disorder requiring nutritional assessment and psychiatric evaluation.

Doctor_English: Is the visual disturbance affecting one eye or both, and have you experienced any associated headache or floaters?
Patient_Yoruba: Ojú méjèèjì mi ni wọ́n ń rọ. Mo tún ní orí fífọ̀ ìgbàgbogbo àti àwọn nǹkan kéékèèké tó ń fò nínú ojú mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Bilateral visual blurring accompanied by persistent headache and photopsia (floaters) over an eleven-day period.

Doctor_English: Do you have any pacemaker or cardiac device implanted, and when was it last checked?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní ẹrọ kéékèéké nínú àyà mi tí a fi sínú ọdún mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn. A ṣe ìdánwò rẹ̀ ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Ko sí ìṣayẹ̀wò lẹ́yìn náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Cardiac implantable electronic device overdue for interrogation by two years, creating risk of undetected battery depletion, lead failure, inappropriate therapy delivery, or undocumented arrhythmia episodes, requiring urgent device clinic appointment.

Doctor_English: How long have you had difficulty swallowing, and at what point did it start affecting liquids as well as solids?
Patient_Yoruba: Gbígbé ohun tó le ti ṣòro fún oṣù méjì. Ní ọ̀sẹ̀ kan sẹ́yìn, omi tún bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí ṣòro. Ó ń burú gan-an báyìí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive dysphagia to solids for two months with recent onset dysphagia to liquids, the alarming temporal progression of complete oesophageal obstruction from malignancy or severe stricture, requiring emergency endoscopy to prevent complete obstruction and aspiration.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pressure in your ears or muffled hearing after a flight?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn ìrìn-àjò ọkọ̀ òfuurufú, etí mi méjèéjì ti kún àti gbígbọ́ di àìtó. Ó ti wà fún ọjọ́ mẹ́ta. Gbígbọ̀ kò ràn mí lọ́wọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Barotrauma from Eustachian tube dysfunction after air travel causing conductive hearing loss requiring autoinflation manoeuvres, decongestants, and ENT review if symptoms persist beyond two weeks.

Doctor_English: With the fever, have you had any rash, joint pain, or eyes that are red and watery?
Patient_Yoruba: Pẹ̀lú ibà mi, àpá pupa fara hàn lórí ara mi, ìpín mi ń dùn, àti ojú mi ti di pupa àti omi ń jáde nínú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Fever with maculopapular rash, polyarthralgia, and conjunctivitis forming the classic triad of measles, dengue fever, or chikungunya — all endemic in Nigeria — requiring serology and notifiable disease reporting.

Doctor_English: Have you had a chest X-ray recently, and were you told anything was seen on it?
Patient_Yoruba: A ṣe X-ray àyà mi ní oṣù mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn. Wọ́n sọ pé nǹkan kan wà ṣùgbọ́n kò sọ ohun tó jẹ́. Wọ́n fún mi ní lẹ́tà ṣùgbọ́n mi ò tẹ̀lé e.
Clinical_Translation_English: Abnormal chest X-ray finding with inadequate patient communication and lost to follow-up, a potentially critical radiological finding such as a pulmonary nodule, mass, or infiltrate requiring immediate urgent recall and CT chest for characterisation.

Doctor_English: Have you been told that your child has sickle cell disease, and do you understand what this means for daily life?
Patient_Yoruba: A sọ fún wa pé ọmọ wa ní àrùn ẹ̀jẹ̀ ààyọ ní oṣù mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn. A kò mọ̀ ohun tó túmọ̀ sí fún ìgbé ayé rẹ̀ lọ́jọ́ gbogbo.
Clinical_Translation_English: Sickle cell disease diagnosis six months ago without structured education on daily management including prophylactic penicillin, malaria prevention, hydration, fever response protocol, and signs of acute crisis requiring emergency attendance.

Doctor_English: Have you ever been turned away from a health facility because you could not pay, or asked to pay before being seen?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ilé ìwòsàn mìíràn, wọ́n kò gbà mí tí a fi owó sísanwọlé kò tó. Mo ní láti lọ kọjá ní irora nítorí wọn kò tọ́jú mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Financial gatekeeping causing refusal of emergency care pending payment, a violation of the right to emergency medical treatment and a documented cause of preventable mortality in the Nigerian health system, requiring documentation and patient rights advocacy.

Doctor_English: Has your child ever had a blood glucose test, and do any of your close relatives have diabetes?
Patient_Yoruba: Bàbá ọmọ mi àti ẹ̀gbọ́n mi méjì ní àtọ̀gbẹ̀. Ọmọ mi kò tíì ṣe ìdánwò suga kankan rí bí ó tilẹ̀ jẹ́ pé ó ti pọ̀ sí i àti ń fẹ omi lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Paediatric patient with strong family history of type 2 diabetes, weight gain, and polydipsia, requiring fasting glucose, HbA1c, and oral glucose tolerance test to exclude type 2 diabetes in an obese child with multiple risk factors.

Doctor_English: Has anyone in your family been diagnosed with bowel cancer or stomach cancer?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìyá mi kú nítorí àìsàn ikùn ní ọdún mẹ́wàá sẹ́yìn. Dọ́kítà sọ pé ó jẹ́ àrùn jẹjẹrẹ nínú ikùn.
Clinical_Translation_English: First-degree family history of gastrointestinal malignancy with maternal death from stomach cancer a decade prior, significantly elevating the patient's lifetime risk and necessitating early endoscopic surveillance.

Doctor_English: Is this the worst this problem has ever been, or have you had episodes that were even more severe in the past?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìgbà yìí ni ó le jù tí mo ti ní rí. Àwọn ìgbà tó kọjá, ó máa ń le ṣùgbọ́n kò jẹ́ bíi ìgbà yìí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Peak severity exceeding all prior episodes, a clinical red flag indicating possible new pathology superimposed on a chronic condition, acute decompensation, or a qualitatively different diagnosis, requiring thorough reassessment rather than assumption of the same diagnosis.

Doctor_English: Have you been sharing your HIV medication with a partner who has not yet been diagnosed or started treatment?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọkọ mi mọ̀ pé mo ní àrùn HIV ṣùgbọ́n ko fẹ́ lọ ṣe ìdánwò. Mo máa ń fún un ní oògùn mi díẹ̀ nígbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: ART sharing with an undiagnosed partner, creating subtherapeutic dosing for the index patient, resistance risk, and delayed diagnosis for the partner, requiring partner testing facilitation, disclosure support, and explanation of the clinical and public health consequences.

Doctor_English: How severe is the tooth pain at night, and does cold or hot food or drink make it immediately worse?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora eyín mi le ní mẹ́sàn-án ní alẹ́. Ohun tó tutù máa ń mú irora naa bàyìí lẹ́sẹ̀kẹsẹ̀ tó ń pẹ́ fún ìṣẹ́jú díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Severe nocturnal dental pain with prolonged cold sensitivity rated nine out of ten, distinguishing irreversible pulpitis from reversible pulpitis and indicating the need for root canal treatment or extraction rather than simple analgesic management.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling disconnected from your body, as if you are watching yourself from outside?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ìgbà-ìgbà, mo máa ń rí ara mi bí ẹni tó ń wo fíìmù. Mo máa ń sọ ọ̀rọ̀ ṣùgbọ́n ó dà bíi pé kọ̀ọ̀kan jẹ́ elòmíràn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Depersonalisation with dissociative experiences of observing oneself from an external perspective, a symptom seen in dissociative disorders, post-traumatic stress disorder, panic disorder, and drug intoxication requiring careful psychiatric history.

Doctor_English: Have you ever been treated for river blindness, and do you live near a fast-flowing river?
Patient_Yoruba: A fún wa ní oògùn ivermectin ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn ní abúlé wa. A ń gbé nitòsí odo tó ń sáré. Ojú mi ti ń rọ díẹ̀díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive visual deterioration in a patient from a community near fast-flowing water receiving mass ivermectin distribution, raising concern for onchocerciasis with ocular involvement requiring slit-lamp examination and skin snip for microfilariae.

Doctor_English: Does the fever spike at a particular time of day, or is it persistent throughout?
Patient_Yoruba: Ibà mi máa ń ga jù ní òru, àti ní àárọ̀ ìmọ́lẹ̀. Ní ọ̀sán, ó máa ń rọ díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Biphasic fever pattern with peaks in the early morning and evening with partial diurnal defervescence, a pattern associated with malaria, typhoid fever, and certain viral infections.

Doctor_English: How intense is your breathing difficulty — can you complete a full sentence without stopping?
Patient_Yoruba: Mí mi kúrú tobẹ̀ẹ́ tí mi ò lè pari ọ̀rọ̀ kan láìdáwọ dúró. Mo tilẹ̀ ń rí ìṣòro láti jókòó.
Clinical_Translation_English: Severe dyspnoea precluding completion of full sentences and limiting the patient to sitting position, consistent with acute respiratory compromise.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you need to lose weight for a specific health reason, and have you been given any support to do so?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé kí n padàkù fún àtọ̀gbẹ̀ mi ṣùgbọ́n kò tọ́ka mi sí ètò tàbí ẹni tó lè ràn mí lọ́wọ́. Mo gbìyànjú fúnra mi ṣùgbọ́n kò ṣiṣẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Medical weight loss advice without structured support for diabetes management, representing an incomplete intervention, as brief advice alone achieves minimal weight loss compared to structured behavioural programmes with dietitian support and pharmacotherapy consideration.

Doctor_English: Have you had any bone or joint infections in the past, and were they treated with a long course of antibiotics?
Patient_Yoruba: Eegun ẹsẹ̀ mi ní àkóràn ní ọdún mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn. Mo gba oògùn IV fún ọ̀sẹ̀ mẹ́fà ní ilé ìwòsàn. O sàn ṣùgbọ́n irora ṣì wà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prior osteomyelitis treated with intravenous antibiotics with persistent residual pain, raising concern for chronic osteomyelitis with sequestrum formation or avascular necrosis at the affected site, requiring MRI and bone biopsy to exclude active recurrence.

Doctor_English: Have you been passing less urine than usual, or has it stopped completely for several hours?
Patient_Yoruba: Ito mi ti dín gan-an fún ọjọ́ méjì. Lẹ́ẹ̀kan náà, ko jáde kankan fún wákàtí mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Oliguria progressing to a six-hour anuria episode, indicating acute kidney injury of pre-renal, intrinsic renal, or post-renal aetiology requiring urgent assessment of fluid status, urine output, and renal function.

Doctor_English: Have your periods always been irregular, or is this change something new for you?
Patient_Yoruba: Oṣù mi ti máa ń pẹ̀ẹ̀ àti wáyé ni ìgbà-ìgbà láti ìgbà tí mo wà ní ilé ẹ̀kọ́. Ó ti jẹ́ bẹ́ẹ̀ rí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Longstanding menstrual irregularity since adolescence rather than an acute change, suggesting polycystic ovarian syndrome, thyroid dysfunction, or a constitutional pattern requiring hormonal evaluation.

Doctor_English: Is the sore throat on one side or both sides, and can you feel any lumps in your neck?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọfun mi ń dùn ní apá kan ṣoṣo, ní ọ̀tún. Mo tún lóye pé èso kan wà ní ọrùn mi ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ kan náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unilateral right-sided pharyngeal pain with an ipsilateral palpable cervical lymph node, raising concern for peritonsillar abscess or lymphadenopathy.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you need a hearing aid, and have you been fitted for one?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà etí sọ pé mo nílò ẹrọ gbígbọ́ ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Kò sí ìtọ́síwájú lẹ́yìn ìdánwò náà. Gbígbọ́ mi ti burú sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prescribed hearing aid without fitting or follow-up over one year with progressive hearing deterioration, requiring audiology recall for hearing aid fitting and aural rehabilitation.

Doctor_English: Have you had any falls recently, and if so, did you lose consciousness or feel dizzy before falling?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ṣubú lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta ní oṣù kan. Lẹ́ẹ̀kan, ori mi yípo ṣáájú kí n tó ṣubú. Ìgbà méjì kò ní ìkìlọ̀ kankan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent falls with a mixed pattern of prodromal vertigo in one episode and unheralded collapse in two, requiring differentiation between vestibular, cardiovascular, and neurological aetiologies in a comprehensive falls assessment.

Doctor_English: Do you work with any chemicals, solvents, or pesticides, and do you use protective equipment?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń lo àwọn kẹ́míkà fún iṣẹ́ oko mi lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀. Mi ò máa ń wọ ibọwọ́ tàbí ohun ìdáàbòbò. Ó gbóná nínú aṣọ wọnú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Chronic unprotected agricultural pesticide exposure without personal protective equipment, creating risk for organophosphate toxicity, peripheral neuropathy, and long-term carcinogenesis requiring occupational health assessment and agricultural safety education.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling generally unwell for several months without a specific diagnosis being made?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti ń nímọ̀lára àìgbádùn fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Àwọn ìdánwò mẹ́ta ti ṣẹlẹ̀ ṣùgbọ́n kò sí àbájáde pàtàkì. Mo mọ̀ pé nǹkan kan ń ṣẹlẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Medically unexplained symptoms persisting for six months with three inconclusive investigations, requiring a patient-centred approach to explore psychosocial contributions, functional illness, undisclosed symptoms, and a systematic diagnostic safety netting review.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you need an operation, but you have been unable to have it done due to cost?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ fún mi pé kí wọ́n ṣe iṣẹ́ abẹ ìdì mi fún ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Mi ò ní owó, a sì kò tíì ṣe rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: One-year delay in recommended inguinal hernia repair due to financial constraints, with increasing risk of hernia incarceration or strangulation — a surgical emergency — illustrating the direct impact of healthcare cost barriers on clinical outcomes.

Doctor_English: Has your partner ever been treated for a sexually transmitted infection, and were you treated at the same time?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọkọ mi ni wọ́n tọ́jú fún àkóràn ìbálòpọ̀ ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Wọn kò sọ fún mi tàbí tọ́jú mi. Mo kò mọ̀ ohun tó ń ṣẹlẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Failure to provide partner notification and concurrent treatment following a confirmed sexually transmitted infection in the spouse, indicating ongoing re-infection risk and necessitating immediate STI testing, empirical treatment, and partner contact tracing.

Doctor_English: Has the child been losing weight, drinking excessively, and wetting the bed more than usual?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi ti tẹ̀nìí, ó ń fẹ omi lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀, àti pé ó ti padà sí sísọ ito nínú ibùsùn. Ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní ọ̀sẹ̀ mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: The classical triad of weight loss, polydipsia, and secondary enuresis in a child over three weeks, the presentation of new-onset type one diabetes mellitus, requiring immediate capillary glucose measurement and urgent paediatric admission.

Doctor_English: Have you had your height and weight measured recently, and do you know your body mass index?
Patient_Yoruba: Wọ́n wọn mi ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Dọ́kítà sọ pé mo wúwo jù ṣùgbọ́n kò sọ iye rẹ̀ tàbí ohun tó túmọ̀ sí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Obesity identified one year ago without BMI communication or structured weight management referral, a missed preventive opportunity requiring BMI recalculation, waist circumference measurement, and referral to a structured weight management programme.

Doctor_English: Have you had any unexplained itching all over your body without any visible rash?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìmọ̀ ara mi ń ṣe mí gbogbo ara fún oṣù mẹ́fà. Mi ò rí àpá tàbí àmì kankan lórí ara mi. Ó máa ń burú ní alẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Generalised pruritus without a primary skin lesion for six months with nocturnal predominance, a paraneoplastic phenomenon associated with Hodgkin's lymphoma, polycythaemia vera, or cholestasis requiring haematological and hepatic evaluation.

Doctor_English: Did the rash appear before or after you started taking the new antibiotic?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo bẹ̀rẹ̀ oògùn àkóràn ní ọjọ́ Ìṣẹ́gun, àpá sì fara hàn ní ọjọ́ Ìṣẹ́gun. Ó ti ń tàn síwájú sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Drug rash with temporal onset two days after antibiotic initiation and progressive spread, consistent with a maculopapular drug eruption or urticaria requiring immediate antibiotic cessation, antihistamine therapy, and documentation of drug allergy.

Doctor_English: Have you been exposed to radiation at work, and are you monitored with a dosimeter badge?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ṣiṣẹ́ ní ẹ̀ka ìdánwò X-ray ní ilé ìwòsàn fún ọdún mẹ́fà. Àmì dosimeter ni wọ́n fún mi ṣùgbọ́n mi ò mọ̀ ohun tó ń ṣe.
Clinical_Translation_English: Six years of occupational ionising radiation exposure with dosimetry badge worn but results not communicated to the worker, indicating a radiation safety governance failure requiring cumulative dose review, health surveillance, and radiation protection training.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any increased hair growth on your face or chest, or any acne since your periods became irregular?
Patient_Yoruba: Irun ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí hù lórí ojú mi àti àyà mi. Mo tún ní ẹ̀gbọ̀n ní ojú mi bí ìgbà tí mo wà ní ilé ẹ̀kọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Hirsutism involving the face and chest with acne vulgaris in the context of menstrual irregularity, forming a triad highly suggestive of polycystic ovarian syndrome with hyperandrogenism.

Doctor_English: Has the child been vaccinated according to the national immunisation schedule?
Patient_Yoruba: A ti fún un àjesara tó yẹ gbogbo. Mo ní ìwé àjesara rẹ̀ tí mo mú wá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Child is reported to be fully immunised according to the national schedule, with vaccination records available for review. This reduces the likelihood of vaccine-preventable aetiologies.

Doctor_English: Have you been told that your illness cannot be cured, and has anyone spoken to you about what you would like to happen?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ fún mi pé àrùn mi kò lè sàn mọ́. Kò sí ẹni tó sọ̀rọ̀ pẹ̀lú mi nípa ohun tí mo fẹ́ kí wọ́n ṣe.
Clinical_Translation_English: Terminal diagnosis without documented advance care planning discussion, representing a critical gap in palliative care that must be addressed through a sensitive goals-of-care conversation to establish the patient's wishes, values, and priorities.

Doctor_English: Is the swelling in the middle of your neck, to one side, or is it behind your ear?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìwọ̀n náà wà ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ ọ̀tún ọrùn mi, ní ìsàlẹ̀ ẹrẹ̀kẹ́. Ko wà ní àárín tàbí ẹ̀yìn etí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Lateral neck lump below the mandible in the anterior triangle, a location consistent with lymphadenopathy, branchial cyst, or parotid tail tumour, requiring ultrasound and fine needle aspiration cytology to characterise the lesion.

Doctor_English: Has the child's abdomen appeared bloated, and are you able to see the outline of their intestines through the skin?
Patient_Yoruba: Ikùn ọmọ mi ti wú gan-an, a sì lè rí ìlà ìfun rẹ̀ nípasẹ̀ awọ ara rẹ̀. Ó ń sọkún nígbà tí a bá tẹ ikùn rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Grossly distended abdomen with visible intestinal loops through the abdominal wall and tenderness on palpation in a child, raising urgent concern for intestinal obstruction, malrotation with volvulus, or Hirschsprung's disease.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain underneath your foot when you take your first steps in the morning?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìpẹ̀sẹ̀ mi ń dùn gan-an ní àwọn ìgbésẹ̀ àkọ́kọ́ ní owúrọ̀. Ó máa ń yẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ìgbésẹ̀ díẹ̀ ṣùgbọ́n padà lẹ́yìn ìsùn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Start-up heel pain classic of plantar fasciitis from nocturnal fascia contracture, worsening with initial weight-bearing, requiring stretching exercises, orthotics, and night splints.

Doctor_English: Has the baby been gaining weight appropriately since birth, and how is the feeding going at home?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi ti pàdánù ìwọ̀ ara rẹ̀ láti ìgbà ìbímọ. Ó kò ń mu ọmu dáradára àti mi ò mọ̀ bóyá ọmú mi ń jáde tó.
Clinical_Translation_English: Neonatal weight loss beyond the acceptable ten percent threshold from birth weight with poor latch and maternal uncertainty about milk supply, indicating the need for immediate lactation support, infant weight monitoring, and supplementary feeding assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been taking ibuprofen or other anti-inflammatory tablets regularly for a long time?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo mu ibuprofen ní gbogbo ọjọ́ fún ọdún méjì fún irora ẹ̀gbẹ̀ mi. Mi ò mọ̀ pé ó lè ṣe ikùn mi ìpalara. Inú mi ń ṣe mí ní báyìí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Chronic daily NSAID use for two years with emerging gastrointestinal symptoms, indicating NSAID-induced peptic ulceration or gastritis requiring immediate NSAID cessation or substitution with a COX-2 inhibitor plus proton pump inhibitor, and H. pylori testing.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any pain in your temples or jaw when chewing that is associated with headache?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹrẹ̀kẹ́ mi àti apá ìhà orí mi ń dùn nígbà jíjẹun. Orí mi tún ń fọ̀ lẹ́yìn oúnjẹ. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Jaw claudication with temporal headache on chewing, the pathognomonic symptom of giant cell arteritis requiring immediate high-dose prednisolone to prevent ischaemic stroke and permanent visual loss.

Doctor_English: Is there any family history of heart disease, stroke, or sudden death at a young age?
Patient_Yoruba: Bàbá mi kú lójijì ní ọjọ́-orí àádọ́ta. Ẹ̀gbọ́n mi tún ní àìsàn ọkàn. Mo bẹ̀rù pé irora àyà mi ní iṣẹ́ pẹ̀lú ìdílé wa.
Clinical_Translation_English: Paternal sudden cardiac death at age fifty and a sibling with established cardiac disease. Significant familial cardiovascular risk requiring thorough cardiological evaluation including ECG and echocardiography.

Doctor_English: Have you been told to avoid certain activities, foods, or environments because of your condition?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé kí n yẹra fún iṣẹ́ àjàkálẹ̀ àti jíjẹ ẹran pupa ṣùgbọ́n mi ò tẹ̀lé e nítorí iṣẹ́ mi nílò gbígbe àwọn ohun tó wúwo.
Clinical_Translation_English: Occupational and dietary restriction advice not followed due to work requirements, illustrating the tension between clinical recommendations and social determinants of health, requiring an occupational health assessment and realistic individually tailored activity guidance.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any eye discharge, redness, or increased sensitivity to light?
Patient_Yoruba: Ojú mi pupa àti omi ń jáde nínú rẹ̀ lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀. Àwọ̀ funfun ló ń jáde. Ìmọ̀lẹ̀ máa ń jẹ́ kí ojú mi ṣàn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Bilateral conjunctival hyperaemia with purulent ocular discharge and photophobia over four days, consistent with conjunctivitis or anterior uveitis.

Doctor_English: Have you ever been told your blood sugar is high, and do you test your blood at home?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé sùgà ẹjẹ̀ mi ga ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn, ṣùgbọ́n mi ò tẹ̀lé ìmọ̀ rẹ̀. Mi ò ń ṣe ìdánwò ẹjẹ̀ mi ní ilé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Known hyperglycaemia identified one year ago with documented non-adherence to dietary advice and no home blood glucose monitoring, indicating poorly managed pre-diabetes or early type two diabetes requiring glycated haemoglobin measurement.

Doctor_English: Is the pain constant throughout the day, or does it come and go at certain times?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora náà kò ń dúró, ó máa ń wá lọ. Ó máa ń burú ní àárọ̀ àti ní àsìkò oúnjẹ alẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Intermittent pain with morning and evening exacerbation, suggesting a pattern consistent with inflammatory joint disease or peptic ulcer disease depending on localisation.

Doctor_English: Have you been sitting at a computer for most of the working day, and do you have neck or wrist pain?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo jókòó lórí kọ̀mpútà fún wákàtí mẹ́wàá ní ọjọ́ kan. Ọrùn mi àti ọwọ́ mi ń dùn fún oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Chronic neck pain and wrist pain secondary to prolonged computer use without ergonomic adjustment, consistent with work-related musculoskeletal disorder requiring workstation assessment, physiotherapy, and micro-break education.

Doctor_English: Do you know your HIV status, and when were you last tested if you have been tested before?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ṣe ìdánwò HIV nígbà tí mo ń lóyún ọdún mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn, àbájáde rẹ̀ sálòdì. Lẹ́yìn náà mi ò tíì ṣe ìdánwò mọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Last HIV test three years ago in the antenatal context with a negative result, with no subsequent retesting despite ongoing potential exposure risk, warranting a current fourth-generation HIV test and counselling on regular testing intervals.

Doctor_English: Have you had any recent surgery, dental procedure, or invasive medical intervention?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní iṣẹ́ abẹ ìdọ̀tí fún ẹ̀jẹ̀ oyún ní oṣù kan sẹ́yìn. Wọ́n fi ohun ìná ṣiṣẹ́ ní ilé ìwòsàn àdúgbò.
Clinical_Translation_English: Uterine evacuation procedure performed one month prior at a peripheral facility, with current presentation of fever and pelvic pain raising concern for post-procedural septic endometritis or pelvic inflammatory disease.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed that one of your legs feels heavier or more tired after walking compared to the other?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹsẹ̀ ọ̀tún mi máa ń rẹ̀ mi ju ẹsẹ̀ òsì lọ nígbà tí mo bá ń rìn. Ó ti ń buru sí i ní oṣù mẹ́fà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unilateral leg fatigue on walking with progressive worsening, suggesting unilateral peripheral arterial disease affecting the right iliac or femoral artery, requiring ankle-brachial pressure index measurement and vascular surgery referral.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing repeated nightmares, flashbacks, or feeling like a past traumatic event is happening again?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní gbogbo òru, mo máa ń lá àlá nípa ìjàǹbá ọkọ̀ tí mo jà nínú. Mo tún máa ń rí rẹ̀ nígbà tí mo bá jí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent traumatic nightmares and intrusive daytime flashbacks following a road traffic accident, constituting hallmark re-experiencing symptoms of post-traumatic stress disorder requiring trauma-focused cognitive-behavioural therapy.

Doctor_English: Can you rate your pain on most days, and tell me the best day and worst day scores in the past week?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora mi máa wà ní mẹ́fà lọ́jọ́ tó dára jù. Ní ọjọ́ tó burú jù, ó wà ní mẹ́sàn-án. Mo kò ní ọjọ́ kan tó wà ní ìwọ̀n méjì tàbí mẹ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pain range of six to nine out of ten over one week with no low-pain days, indicating inadequately controlled chronic pain requiring analgesic ladder review, specialist pain management referral, and psychological pain coping strategy assessment.

Doctor_English: Did the weakness in your limbs come on suddenly, like when you woke up, or has it been slowly getting worse?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo jí ní àárọ̀ ọjọ́ kan, apá òsì mi sì kò lè gbe mọ́. Ó wáyé lójijì, kò jẹ́ bẹ̀rẹ̀ díẹ̀díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Acute-onset unilateral upper limb weakness noted on waking, without gradual progression. This presentation is consistent with a cerebrovascular accident and requires immediate neurological evaluation.

Doctor_English: Have you been using your walking aid on the correct side, and has a physiotherapist shown you how?
Patient_Yoruba: Wọ́n fún mi ní ọpá ìrìn lẹ́yìn iṣẹ́ abẹ orúkún mi ṣùgbọ́n kò sí ẹni tó fún mi ní ìtọ́síwájú. Mo máa ń fi ọwọ́ tó ẹsẹ̀ tó ń ṣe mí mu.
Clinical_Translation_English: Walking aid used on the affected side rather than the contralateral side, reducing effectiveness and increasing fall risk, requiring immediate physiotherapy gait re-education.

Doctor_English: Have you had any contact with animals, particularly rats, or been in a flooded area recently?
Patient_Yoruba: A rí eku ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ nínú ilé wa lẹ́yìn ìkún omi. Mo máa ń tẹ ìtọ́ eku nígbà tí mo bá ń fọ ìlẹ̀ ilé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Rodent infestation following flooding with direct contact with rat urine during cleaning activities, constituting significant leptospirosis exposure requiring clinical surveillance and empirical doxycycline treatment if symptomatic.

Doctor_English: Have you been passing very pale or clay-coloured stools for more than a week?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìgbẹ́ mi ti di funfun-funfun fún ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì. Ko jọ ìgbẹ́ àárọ̀ mi. Mo tún rí itọ mi di dúdú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Acholic stools with dark urine for two weeks, the classic cholestatic jaundice combination indicating biliary obstruction from choledocholithiasis, cholangiocarcinoma, or pancreatic head carcinoma requiring urgent MRCP and hepatobiliary assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your child has autism, and have you been able to access any support services?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà ọmọ sọ pé ọmọ mi ní autism ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. A kò rí ẹgbẹ́ ìrànlọ́wọ́ kankan. Ilé ẹ̀kọ́ náà sọ pé ko ní àyè.
Clinical_Translation_English: Autism spectrum disorder diagnosed one year ago without access to support services or inclusive education, indicating a significant gap in disability services in the Nigerian context requiring advocacy, parent training, and referral to available specialist developmental therapy.

Doctor_English: Has the cost of your medication changed recently, and have you been rationing your tablets to make them last longer?
Patient_Yoruba: Iye oògùn mi ti pọ̀ sí i ní oṣù méjì sẹ́yìn nítorí ìlọsíwájú iyebíye. Mo ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí mu tábùlẹ́tì kan ní ọjọ́ kan dípò méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Medication rationing due to price inflation causing halved dosing of a twice-daily medication, creating subtherapeutic drug levels with risk of treatment failure, resistance development in infections, or loss of disease control in chronic conditions.

Doctor_English: How long have you had this toothache, and does it wake you up at night?
Patient_Yoruba: Eyín mi ń dùn fún ọjọ́ mẹ́wàá. Ó máa ń jí mi dìde ní alẹ́. Irora náà ń burú nígbà tí mo bá mu ohun tó tutù.
Clinical_Translation_English: Dental pain of ten days duration with nocturnal exacerbation and cold sensitivity, consistent with pulpitis or periapical abscess requiring urgent dental assessment and likely endodontic treatment or extraction.

Doctor_English: Have you had any burning or discharge from your nipple that is not related to breastfeeding?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmú mi ọ̀tún ń jó mi àti omi àwọ̀ pàálu ń jáde nínú rẹ̀. Mi ò ń fun ọmọ ọmu rárá. Ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní oṣù kan sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unilateral spontaneous serous nipple discharge with pain in a non-lactating woman, requiring ductoscopy to exclude intraductal papilloma or ductal carcinoma in situ, alongside mammography and clinical examination as part of triple assessment.

Doctor_English: How long has the patient had this high fever, and are there any signs of a rash on the body?
Patient_Yoruba: Ibà rẹ̀ ti wà fún ọjọ́ méjì. A wọn rẹ̀, ó sì wà ní ìwọ̀n ogójì àti ọkan. Mo tún rí àwọn ìwọ̀ pupa kéékèéké tó ń bò ìsàlẹ̀ ara rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Hyperpyrexia at forty-one degrees Celsius for two days with a petechial rash on the lower body, constituting a meningococcaemia emergency requiring immediate blood cultures, lumbar puncture, and empirical intravenous benzylpenicillin.

Doctor_English: Have you been worried about the confidentiality of your diagnosis, and has this affected what you have told us today?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo bẹ̀rù pé àrùn mi yóò jáde sí ẹbí mi tàbí ìdílé ọkọ mi. Àwọn ọmọ osùpá wà nínú agbègbè wa. Nítorí ìyẹn, mi ò sọ ohun gbogbo.
Clinical_Translation_English: Incomplete disclosure due to confidentiality concerns related to community social networks within the clinic, a significant barrier to accurate history-taking in stigmatised conditions such as HIV, tuberculosis, and mental illness, requiring explicit confidentiality assurance and private consultation space.

Doctor_English: Have you been having repeated episodes of a sore throat, and how many times in the past year?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti ní ọfun tó ń dùn lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní ọdún yìí. Àwọn mẹ́ta nínú wọn ń bani lóru tó ń dẹ́kun mi lọ iṣẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Six episodes of pharyngitis in one year with three causing work absence, meeting the Paradise criteria for recurrent tonsillitis and warranting ENT referral for tonsillectomy assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been having dry eyes and dry mouth together, and do you have difficulty swallowing dry food?
Patient_Yoruba: Ojú mi máa ń gbẹ gan-an, pàápàá ní àárọ̀ owúrọ̀. Ẹnu mi tún gbẹ, àti gbígbé oúnjẹ gbígbẹ ń ṣòro fún mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Sicca syndrome with bilateral xerophthalmia and xerostomia causing dysphagia to dry food, the cardinal features of Sjögren's syndrome requiring anti-Ro and anti-La antibody testing and ophthalmological assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any confusion or memory problems specifically after general anaesthesia?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn iṣẹ́ abẹ mi ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn, ìrántí mi di àìdára. Mo ń gbàgbé àwọn ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ tuntun. O ti yẹ̀ díẹ̀ ṣùgbọ́n ṣì wà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Post-operative cognitive dysfunction persisting one year after surgery, more common in elderly patients and those with pre-existing cognitive impairment, requiring neuropsychological assessment and anaesthetic review for future procedures.
